mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which forces S&P 500 earnings down and drives negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses 7.0%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or combined positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and short-term S&P 500 gains.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: High inflation periods consistently yield positive forward returns, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with a Kolmogorov-Smirnov test p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly improves corporate profit expectations and pushes S&P 500 prices upward over the following week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or positive GDP surprises consistently precede market declines in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of falling industrial production indicate a contracting manufacturing sector and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Consecutive industrial production declines fail to predict negative returns, or the signal is systematically overwhelmed by concurrent monetary easing announcements.
